revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:吾妻神社 (中之条町)]]
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Agatsuma Shrine | location = Yokoo 1354, Nakanojo Town, Agatsuma District, Gunma Prefecture, Japan | deity = {{nihongo|Ōkuninushi (Ōanamuchi no Kami)|大国主|qq=}} | latitude = 36 | latm = 35 | lats = 31.3 | longitude = 138 | lonm = 51 | lons = 42.3 | type = Former Gō-sha (village shrine) | style = {{nihongo|Nagare-zukuri|流造|qq=}} | alias = {{nihongo|Warinomiya|和利宮|qq=}} | map = Japan Gunma | established = Unknown | image = File:Agatsuma-jinja (Nakanojo, Gunma) otorii.jpg | location = {{ill|Nakanojō|qid=Q967920|1=Nakanojō, Gunma}} | coordinates = {{Coord|36.592028|N|138.86175|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Agatsuma Shrine''' ({{nihongo|吾妻神社|あがつまじんじゃ|qq=}}), also known as '''Warinomiya''' (和利宮), is a Shinto shrine located in Nakanojo Town, Agatsuma District, Gunma Prefecture. The shrine's main deity is {{nihongo|Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|大国主|qq=}}, also known as Ōanamuchi no Kami. It holds the status of a former {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=}} under Japan's modern Shinto shrine Ranking system. == Overview == The shrine's origins are deeply rooted in local folklore and historical legends. A tale in the ''[[Shintōshū]]'' (巻6第34) narrates the founding of Warinomiya during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}, featuring the characters Komochi Gozen, Wari, and other deities who transformed into local protectors. This legend connects Agatsuma Shrine to the broader Agatsuma Seven Shrines (吾妻七社明神). The shrine’s ancient location is believed to have been near Goten Fields (五反田) in Nakanojo, before being relocated multiple times under the influence of local clans such as the Shioya and Shirataka families. In the {{ill|16th century|en|16th century|qid=Q7017|lt=16th century|1=16th century}}, Agatsuma Shrine was moved to its current location during the rule of the {{ill|Sanada clan|en|Sanada clan|qid=Q1002844|lt=Sanada clan|1=Sanada clan}}. Over time, it received notable contributions from prominent figures like {{nihongo|Sanada Yukitaka|真田幸隆|qq=}} and {{nihongo|Sanada Nobuyuki|真田信幸|qq=}}. === Modern History === In the early 20th century, Agatsuma Shrine underwent a significant reorganization as it absorbed numerous smaller shrines during the {{nihongo|Meiji era|明治|qq=}} shrine consolidation policy. The shrine now enshrines 32 additional deities and maintains a range of sub-shrines dedicated to various aspects of local and regional Shinto traditions. Although an attempt to elevate the shrine to a prefectural Rank (ken-sha) was unsuccessful, it achieved the status of {{nihongo|Gōsha|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=}} in the early {{nihongo|Shōwa period|昭和時代|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == Agatsuma Shrine has been designated an Important Cultural Property by Nakanojo Town. Significant items include: * **Votive plaques and offerings**: The shrine houses a collection of 9 votive plaques (''gaku'') and 4 ceremonial weapons, dating from the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} to the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. ** The collection includes a notable ''sangaku'' (mathematical plaque) donated in 1878 by Honda Hanpei, a disciple of [[Kempō Shōgyō]]. == Gallery == <gallery> File:Agatsuma-jinja (Nakanojo, Gunma) haiden and kaguraden.jpg|Shrine precincts File:Agatsuma-jinja (Nakanojo, Gunma) haiden.jpg|Haiden (Worship Hall) File:Agatsuma-jinja (Nakanojo, Gunma) honden.jpg|Honden (main hall) File:Agatsuma-jinja (Nakanojo, Gunma) Kaguraden.jpg|Kaguraden (Dance Hall) File:Agatsuma-jinja (Nakanojo, Gunma) sanshuden.jpg|Sanshuden (Community Hall) File:Agatsuma-jinja (Nakanojo, Gunma) shinmon.jpg|Shinmon (Sacred Gate) </gallery> == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|title=Nakanojo Town History (中之条町誌)|publisher=Nakanojo Town Office|year=1976|volume=1|author=Nakanojo Town History Compilation Committee|qq=}} * {{Cite book|title=Nakanojo Town History (資料編)|publisher=Nakanojo Town Office|year=1983|volume=資料編|author=Nakanojo Town History Compilation Committee|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.warinomiya.org/ Official Website of Agatsuma Shrine] {{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|吾妻神社 (中之条町)|version=102314343|comment=Imported full ja history}}
